mnemonics: SUPER: 'Sending Upwards, Parent's Essentials Retrieved' - It's how a child class reaches up to its parent class to access its methods and constructors.
keyPoints: The `super` keyword is used to call the superclass (parent class) constructor from the subclass (child class) constructor.
It can be used to access superclass methods that have been overridden in the subclass, allowing you to leverage parent class behavior.
A call to `super()` must be the first statement in a subclass constructor if used.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call `super()` in the constructor, which the compiler does automatically only if there's a no-argument constructor in the parent class.
Trying to use `super` to access private members of the parent class, which violates encapsulation and is not allowed.
